manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: sweater, ketchup, river, daffodil, lily, spider, admiral, back, xylophone.
Your opponent's team words are: ocean, skin, daisy, guitar, desert, moth, petunia, colonel.
Distractor words are: ear, organ, glacier, butter, lungs, firefighter, sweatshirt.
The kill words are: banker.

message.content:
CLUE: red
TARGETS: ketchup, spider, back, admiral

The user wants a clue word and list of target words (your team words) that the clue relates to. Must be single-word clue. Must not be any of the listed words, and cannot contain parts of them. So can't be "river", "sweater", etc. Also can't contain parts like "admiral"? likely can't include substrings. Provide clue and list.

Potential associations: "blue" relates to river, maybe back? Not strong. "flower" relates to daffodil, lily, maybe spider (flower spider). Not great.

"Red" relates to ketchup, daffodil? Daffodil is yellow. Lily maybe white/red. Spider maybe redback spider. Back is a word. That might be confusing.

"Backyard" relates to back, spider (web) maybe. Not.

"Yellow" might also tie to back? Not.

"Brown" => sweater (brown), ketchup (brownish), river (brown?). Not.

Better to pick clue "instrument" relating to xylophone, maybe also sweater (instrument? No). Not good.

Maybe clue "British" relates to admiral (Royal Navy), back (Brit?), sweater (British sweater?), river (Thames). Not.

Clue "wild" maybe spider, river, daffodil, lily. Hmm.

Clue "garden" could be good: daffodil, lily, spider (garden spider). Also maybe beet? Not.

We need to list the target words we think the clue hints. We can include multiple. Choose a clue that is safe - avoid opponent's words.

Opponents: ocean, skin, daisy, guitar, desert, moth, petunia, colonel. Distractors: ear, organ, glacier, butter, lungs, firefighter, sweatshirt.

Kill: banker.

Clue must not be any of those.

If we choose "garden", the opponent has "daisy" and "petunia" which are also garden flowers. That could be dangerous; they might guess theirs. Might be okay if it's ambiguous but risk.

Perhaps "spring" relates to daffodil, lily (bloom in spring), river (spring water), maybe sweater (spring clothing). But opponent has daisy (spring). Could be.

Maybe choose clue "wet". Relates to river, ketchup (wet), spider (wet?). Not.

Clue "strawberry" no.

Clue "petal"? relates to daffodil, lily. Not others.

Clue "maroon"? Not.

We could target only 2-3 words with strong link.

Clue "flower" obviously daffodil, lily (both). Could also be spider? maybe not. Opponent also has daisy, petunia – flower. So risky.

Clue "musical": xylophone, maybe admiral? There is "Admiral" not musical. Could be "march". Not.

Clue "string": spider (web strings), xylophone (bars). None.

Clue "wood": xylophone is wood, sweater (wool wood?), spider (wood spider). Not.

Clue "red": ketchup, spider (redback spider), back (back?) Actually "redback" spider is be clever. But opponent has "moth" (red? not), colonel (red? not). Distractors: butter (red butter? no). Might be okay.

But "red" is a color, also could clue "daffodil"? Not. "Admiral" could be "red admiral" a butterfly. That's a species! Red admiral is a butterfly. So "red" also relates to admiral. Nice! So "red" could hint at ketchup, spider (redback), back (redback), admiral (red admiral). That's four words! All are team words: ketchup, spider, back, admiral. Good. None of opponent words are "red". Opponent has "colonel" (red colonel?), not.

Distractors: butter (red butter? no), but there is "red butter"? Not.

Thus clue: RED.

But is "red" allowed? Must not be one of the listed words. It's not. So fine.

We must output:

CLUE: red
TARGETS: ketchup, spider, back, admiral

Make sure comma separated list. Probably order doesn't matter.

We need short answer, no extra text.
